_readme: voksa tuning console — help copy registry. Every '?' affordance in the console resolves its text from this file by key. Empty string = the console shows '// help pending — <key>'. Fill these from Claude Code; plain text, 1-3 short sentences, QUINE voice (dry, precise, deadpan). Field-level keys are SHARED across phonemes/emotions (vt.fields.* applies to every phoneme's F1, att.fields.* to every emotion's pitch shift, etc.).
input.text: The Lojban to speak, in ordinary orthography — voksa applies the CLL rules (syllabification, stress, pauses) itself, so you don't mark them. A period is a pause/glottal stop, a comma is a syllable break (never a pause), an apostrophe is [h].
input.sentences: A curated coverage set — each line exercises a different corner of the phonology: clusters, sibilants, names, numbers, every vowel, every stop. ⟳ cycles through them; picking one also sets its flags.
transcript.line: The engine's own phonetic reading of the text — the exact segments and boundaries it is about to speak, rendered from the same data as the audio. If a render surprises you, read this first: CAPS mark stress, ‖ a pause, (ɪ) an inserted buffer vowel.
flags.flat: Strips all prosody: flat pitch, no stress lengthening or pitch excursion, no declination. The bare segmental voice — useful for judging a phoneme's tuning without prosodic coloring.
flags.xu: Adds a terminal pitch rise to mark a yes/no question (the cmavo xu). It has no effect — and is disabled — when flat is on, since there is no pitch to raise.
flags.dotside: Drops the la / lai / la'i / doi exemption so a pause precedes EVERY name word (cmevla). The stricter 'dot-side' reading of CLL §4.9 — more pauses, unambiguous name boundaries.
flags.buffer: Inserts a short [ɪ] buffer vowel to break up consonant clusters. It is never written and never counts toward stress — an optional pronunciation aid, off by default.
transport.speak: Renders the whole utterance and plays it. The first click also unlocks the browser's audio context (autoplay policy); after that, changes can speak on their own if 'speak on change' is on.
transport.autospeak: Re-speaks about 400 ms after you stop changing something — a slider, the text, a flag. Turn it off to drive playback yourself with ▶ speak.
transport.waveform: The rendered PCM, one peak per column. A quick visual check on silence, clipping, and overall envelope — it is an amplitude trace, not a spectrogram.
transport.wav: Downloads the last render as a 48 kHz mono 16-bit WAV — the exact samples you just heard.
share.export: Downloads a config JSON: your text, flags, and all sixteen knobs always, plus ONLY the phoneme and attitudinal values you changed, plus your notes and the phonetic line. It replays bit-identically via voksa --config.
share.load: Loads a config JSON — use the picker or drag a file onto this card. Loading REPLACES all state (a clean slate, not a merge); a value beyond a slider's range widens the slider instead of clamping.
share.notes: Free text that travels inside the exported JSON. Say what you were listening for and what you judged — the next person to open the config reads this.
share.cta: Your ears are the ground truth. Phoneme and attitudinal values are documented conventions, not facts — if a tuning sounds better to you, send the JSON back as a GitHub issue or email.
presets: Named starting points for the prosody knobs (sing-song, monotone, fast, slow & calm…). Selecting one resets the knobs to its values but leaves your flags alone; touch anything afterward and the label reads 'Custom'.
reset.all: Returns every parameter — prosody, naturalness, attitudinals, and the whole voice table — to the engine defaults. Your text and flags are left as they are.
prosody._section: How pitch, timing, and loudness move across the whole utterance: declination, stress, and the optional question rise. Segment identity lives in the voice table; this is the melody sung over it.
prosody.declination_start_hz: F0 at the start of the utterance, in Hz. Pitch falls linearly from here down to 'pitch end' — the natural declination of a spoken phrase. Default 120 Hz.
prosody.declination_end_hz: F0 at the end of the utterance, in Hz — the floor of the declination line. Keep it below 'pitch start' for a falling contour. Default 95 Hz.
prosody.stress_duration_factor: How much longer a stressed syllable's rhyme runs — a multiplier on the nucleus and coda, while the onset keeps unit rate (Phase 7.1). 1.5× default; higher is more emphatic.
prosody.stress_f0_excursion_hz: Extra Hz added to F0 on a stressed syllable, above the declination line — the pitch accent that makes stress audible as well as longer. Default 20 Hz.
prosody.stress_amp_factor: Amplitude multiplier on a stressed syllable, a gentle loudness bump alongside the length and pitch cues. Default 1.2×.
prosody.xu_rise_hz: How many Hz the terminal question rise climbs when the xu flag is on, applied at the utterance end for a yes/no question. Default 25 Hz.
prosody.rate: Global tempo multiplier on every segment duration. Above 1 is faster, below 1 slower; 1.0 is the tuned baseline. It does not touch pitch.
naturalness._section: Nine subtle, rule-driven imperfections that lift the voice off dead-mechanical — flutter, breath, micro-pitch, final lengthening, coarticulation. All default ON (Phase 11); the A/B latch lets you hear the voice without them.
naturalness.ab: A hears your current naturalness settings; B renders all nine at identity — the plain Phase-10 voice — without changing your stored values. Toggle to hear exactly what the naturalness layer adds.
naturalness.flutter: Slow quasi-random F0 wander (Klatt flutter), in FL units. A little keeps the pitch from sounding frozen; too much sounds unsteady. Default 25.
naturalness.breath_aspiration: Adds a constant breath-noise floor to voiced sound, softening the voice toward breathy. 0 is fully modal. Default 0.06.
naturalness.baseline_oq_delta: Shifts the glottal open quotient from baseline — how long the glottis stays open each cycle. Higher is softer and breathier, lower is pressed and harder. Default +0.1.
naturalness.baseline_tilt_delta: Tilts the source spectrum globally: negative rolls off the highs (darker, warmer), positive brightens. Default −0.1.
naturalness.micro_f0_hz: Intrinsic vowel pitch: high vowels (i, u) sit a few Hz above low vowels (a) — a real, measurable micro-effect. Scaled in Hz. Default 4 Hz.
naturalness.obstruent_f0_hz: A brief F0 kick after an obstruent — voiceless consonants raise the following vowel's onset pitch. Scaled in Hz. Default 6 Hz.
naturalness.final_lengthen: Stretches the last syllable before a pause — phrase-final lengthening, a strong boundary cue. A multiplier; 1.0 disables it. Default 1.3×.
naturalness.cluster_shorten: Shortens consonants inside a cluster, since segments compress when crowded. A ratio; 0 disables it. Default 0.15.
naturalness.undershoot: How far a short vowel's formants fall short of their target when there isn't time to reach it (coarticulatory undershoot). 0 always hits target, 1 is maximum. Default 0.35.
att._section: The UI attitudinals (.ui joy, .oi complaint, .ii fear…) as voice-quality overlays — pitch, tempo, and glottal changes layered onto the word they attach to. Invented and non-normative: the CLL fixes what an attitudinal MEANS, never how it sounds, which is exactly why your tuning matters.
att.emotion.ui: .ui — happiness / joy. voksa's convention: raised, wider pitch with a slightly brighter, more open voice. Edit the eight deviations below; try 'coi munje .ui'.
att.emotion.uu: .uu — pity / sadness. Lowered, narrowed pitch, slower tempo, a breathier source. Try 'mi klama .uu'.
att.emotion.oi: .oi — complaint / pain. A touch low and creaky (diplophonia), slightly dragging. Try 'coi munje .oi'.
att.emotion.ii: .ii — fear. High, unsteady pitch with added vibrato and breath. Try 'coi munje .ii'.
att.emotion.o'o: .o'o — patience / calm. Lowered, flattened pitch, deliberately close to monotone. Try 'mi klama .o'o'.
att.emotion.au: .au — desire. Mildly raised pitch with a little extra breath. Try 'mi djica .au'.
att.emotion.o'onai: .o'onai — anger (.o'o flipped by nai). Raised, wide pitch, pressed and creaky, brighter and harder. Try 'mi fengu .o'onai'.
att.fields.f0_mean_hz: Adds or subtracts Hz from this affect's mean pitch — the whole word shifted up or down. Additive, not a multiplier.
att.fields.f0_range_mult: Scales the pitch excursion within the word: above 1 is more animated, below 1 flatter, 0 monotone.
att.fields.rate_mult: Time-scales the word's segments. Above 1 is slower (dwelling), below 1 faster (clipped); the unit reads '× slower'.
att.fields.oq: Open-quotient shift for this affect: higher is a softer, breathier glottal source, lower is pressed and tense.
att.fields.tilt: Spectral-tilt shift for this affect: positive brightens (harder, tenser), negative darkens (warmer, softer).
att.fields.di: Diplophonia — alternating-cycle excitation that drops a subharmonic at F0/2, the acoustics of creak / vocal fry. 0 is off, 1 full.
att.fields.vibrato_hz: Adds periodic pitch vibrato at this depth in Hz — the waver used for fear and tension. 0 disables it.
att.fields.aspiration: Extra aspiration noise for this affect, added over the baseline breath floor. Additive.
vt._section: Every phoneme's raw acoustics — formants, bandwidths, amplitudes, voicing, durations. This is where segment identity lives; the numbers are documented conventions, not facts, and are exactly what community tuning is for.
vt.changedonly: Dims every keycap you haven't touched (and haven't selected) to 0.25, so the phonemes you are tuning stand out. Nothing is hidden — just quieted.
vt.class.vowels: The six Lojban vowels (a e i o u y=[ə]) as steady formant targets: F1/F2/F3, bandwidths, amplitudes, duration. Diphthongs glide between these, so retuning a vowel also moves its diphthongs.
vt.class.diphthongs: The 16 diphthongs. Only duration is tunable here — the glide endpoints derive from the tuned vowels, so there is nothing else to set.
vt.class.stops: The six stops (p t k b d g), each a closure phase (silence, or a voice bar for b/d/g) then a release burst. Two formant blocks plus closure and burst durations.
vt.class.fricatives: The fricatives (f v s z c j x) as steady turbulence: an F3 band placed and shaped for the noise, plus voicing for the voiced pairs.
vt.class.sonorants: Nasals (m n) and liquids (l r) — fully voiced steady targets with their characteristic low formants and, for the nasals, heavy F1 damping.
vt.class.other: The apostrophe [h] (aspiration shaped by the next vowel — duration only) and the buffer vowel [ɪ] that the --buffer flag inserts inside clusters.
vt.fields.f1_hz: First formant, Hz — the resonance most tied to vowel height: low vowels have a high F1, high vowels a low F1. Seeded targets are in docs/formants.md.
vt.fields.bw1_hz: F1 bandwidth, Hz. Wider is a more damped, duller resonance; narrower is sharper and more ringing.
vt.fields.amp1: F1 amplitude (linear). 0 mutes this formant, ~1 is full — used to voice or silence a resonance per segment.
vt.fields.f2_hz: Second formant, Hz — mostly front/back tongue position: front vowels have a high F2, back vowels a low one. The most identity-bearing formant.
vt.fields.bw2_hz: F2 bandwidth, Hz. Wider damps the resonance.
vt.fields.f3_hz: Third formant, Hz — fine timbre, and for fricatives and stop bursts, where the noise band sits.
vt.fields.bw3_hz: F3 bandwidth, Hz. For fricatives this is wide — a broad noise band rather than a sharp resonance.
vt.fields.voicing: Glottal (voiced) source amount, 0–1. 1 is fully voiced (vowels, sonorants, the b/d/g voice bar), 0 is voiceless (p/t/k, s/f).
vt.fields.aspiration: Aspiration / frication noise amount, 0–1 — the turbulent source. High for fricatives and stop bursts, 0 for clean vowels.
vt.fields.dur_ms: Steady-state duration in milliseconds, before prosody scales it. Diphthongs and [h] expose only this parameter.
vt.fields.closure_ms: Stop closure duration, ms — the silent (or voice-bar) hold before the burst.
vt.fields.burst_ms: Stop release-burst duration, ms — the short noisy transient when the closure opens.
